name: Foundation: Universal Principles
description: The language-agnostic core of the rule catalogue (common/) — agents, code-review, coding-style, development-workflow, git-workflow, hooks, patterns, performance, security, and testing — that every language ruleset inherits and overrides, like the base of a CSS-specificity cascade.

name: TypeScript & Web Core
description: The shared frontend substrate that sits between the universal core and the UI frameworks: the foundational TypeScript/JavaScript ruleset plus the web-domain rules (CSS, semantic HTML, design quality, Core Web Vitals) that react, angular, vue, and nuxt all build on.

name: Frontend Frameworks
description: Component-based UI framework rulesets — React, Angular, Vue, and Nuxt — that extend the TypeScript & Web Core layer with framework-specific component, state, routing, and rendering conventions.

name: JVM Languages
description: Rulesets for the JVM ecosystem — Java and Kotlin (including Android/KMP) — extending the universal core with records, sealed types, coroutines, and JVM-specific tooling and testing standards.

name: .NET Languages
description: Rulesets for the .NET ecosystem — C# and F# — extending the universal core with records/immutability, ASP.NET Core security, and framework-appropriate (xUnit, FsCheck property-based) testing conventions.

name: Systems Languages
description: Rulesets for compiled systems languages — Rust, C++, and Go — extending the universal core with ownership/RAII memory discipline, idiomatic error handling, static-analysis tooling, and race/sanitizer-aware testing.

name: Scripting Languages
description: Rulesets for dynamic scripting and web-backend languages — Python (including the FastAPI framework ruleset), Ruby/Rails, Perl, and PHP — extending the universal core with interpreter-specific style, taint/injection safety, and framework testing conventions.

name: Mobile & Cross-Platform
description: Rulesets for mobile and cross-platform app languages — Swift (Apple platforms), Dart/Flutter, and ArkTS/HarmonyOS — extending the universal core with platform-specific UI patterns, mobile security hardening, and widget/UI testing.

name: Catalogue Index
description: The catalogue's entry point — the top-level README that documents the layered rule hierarchy and points readers to the common core and per-language directories.
